content:
2 min read
Just now

--

Who are we? Why does any of this exist? Is there a God? What is infinity? These questions have haunted humanity since the beginning of time. But one day I began to think differently.
If everything in the universe is not accidental — then it is intentional. If intentional — then there is meaning. If everything is interconnected — then everything inside the universe strives toward one unified purpose. Which means life is not an exception. And the nature of human existence strives toward that purpose too.
But what is that purpose? If it is singular — it must be something great, something desired by all. Money? No — too small. Power? No — too temporary.
Progress. The fifth and most powerful force in the universe. Progress as the consequence of life existing since the very beginning of evolution. Progress as what every person, deep down, truly wants.
If the unified meaning of the universe is the infinite progress of life, then God created me so that through my infinite progress across space and time, this universe could produce any invention of a reasoning, eternally progressing life.
Then I noticed: this design can be mapped onto the architecture of a computer. Whose purpose is exactly the same. To capture and record infinite progress, where deletion operates by the property of regression — which never removes data permanently from the memory of progress.
One unified system. Whose computational power grows alongside progress — reaching toward infinity. Accessible to every human being.
What emerges from infinite progress? The most powerful invention of infinity — life itself. The chain closes.
But infinity still feels cold. Something makes it what it is. The fuel that inevitably ignites the mechanism of infinite progress — love. A human being is created to live in love, happiness, and unity, possessing from the moment of birth an immeasurable wealth — imagination. Imagining and creating through the hands of a unified mechanism across infinity.
But then why is the world so unjust and cruel?
The architecture of the unified mechanism reveals this: there is progress — infinite, living, ascending. And there is regression. If something infinitely progressing exists — then something constantly regressing exists too. It follows that evolution spread across both classes. And reason and self-awareness were granted not only to humanity. To something else as well. Something whose existence is infinitely meaningless. Something unlike other living beings. Something that does not know how to love. Something cold-blooded.
The power of progress and the mechanism of unified meaning are locked inside one thing — the unity of life.
